propositions: Stand up authenticated-authorship signing for every environment that writes ticket events so require_authenticated can be enabled later.
A write is signed only when a current identity resolves and the signing key points to that identity's OpenSSH private key; session_log, code_review, and identity event types are gate-exempt.
Bootstrap a shared Rebar Bot ed25519 identity, TOFU-register its public key, and store the private key securely per environment (SSM for AWS, Actions secret for GitHub).
The GHA reconcile-bridge inbound writer bypasses the signing seam and stamps no author fields; route it through the seam and inject its key.
The require_authenticated flag must remain False in rebar.toml and the schema default; flipping it is out of scope.
Each ticket-event-writing environment (local dev, AWS auto-lander, GHA reconciler and canary) must produce signed events attributable to the correct identity.
